figures.caption: Table 1: Comparison of parameter-free gradient-variation (static) regret; we also include the best-known parameter-free optimistic/problem-independent regret. gt denotes the gradient feedback ∇ft(wt). “Efficient” denotes that the algorithm has a per-round computational cost independent of T , and it is meaningful only when the leading term has a linear-polylogarithmic dependence on ∥u∥.
